qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: OAuth2 dynamic client registration trusts attacker-selected registration endpoints and permits SSRF
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory is a stored XSS issue in rendered Chat Trigger, Form, Send & Wait, Webhook, and Chat node pages, fixed in HTML sanitization and webhook CSP/header paths. This finding instead concerns the OAuth2 credential flow in `credentials.service.ts` and `oauth.service.ts`, where attacker-supplied authorization-server metadata steers outbound requests.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's stored-XSS root cause in rendered node pages. The only qualifying finding is an OAuth2 dynamic client registration SSRF primitive in a different component and control flow.
evidence: The advisory JSON describes stored XSS by an authenticated workflow editor through Form Trigger, Chat Trigger, Send & Wait, Webhook Node, and Chat Node pages, with script execution in another user's browser.

The referenced February 2026 fix commits modify HTML-rendering and response-hardening paths such as `packages/@n8n/nodes-langchain/nodes/trigger/ChatTrigger/templates.ts`, `packages/nodes-base/nodes/Form/utils/utils.ts`, `packages/nodes-base/utils/sendAndWait/utils.ts`, `packages/cli/src/webhooks/webhook-request-handler.ts`, and `packages/cli/src/webhooks/webhook-response-headers.ts`.

The authoritative session manifest at `/Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_7b28eaf2/.judge/input_manifest.json` lists only one final qualifying finding: `oauth2_dynamic_client_registration_ssrf`, whose report targets `/rest/oauth2-credential/auth`, `credentials.service.ts`, `validate-oauth-url.ts`, and `oauth.service.ts` issuing attacker-directed OAuth metadata and registration requests.
